meta.title: X3 Engines — The Complete UK Guide

hero.tagPill: BMW X3 • 2003–Present • 4 Generations (E83, F25, G01, G45) • M54, M57, N52, N47, N20, N55, N57, B47, B48, B58, S58
hero.h1: X3 Engines — The Complete UK Guide
hero.subHeadline: Every BMW X3 engine across four generations — from the M54 straight-six of the E83 to the B48 four-cylinder and B58 straight-six of the G01 and G45. Real UK replacement costs, known failure patterns, and honest ownership economics for BMW's best-selling SUV. Backed by real UK X3 engine enquiries in 2025 [BMW-VERIFIED].

metrics.ourCall: ⚠️ The X3 rule — the SUV premium: a B47/B48 recon at £2,800–£5,000 against a £12,000–£25,000 G01 is 11–42% — ✅ safe. An N47 recon at £3,200–£5,500 against a £4,000–£8,000 E83 is 40–138% — ⚠️ watch to ❌ scrap. Generation decides everything.
★★★★☆ — the B48/B58 are the modern gold standard; the N52 and M57 are robust classics; the N47 and N20 carry the chain-tensioner caveat
B58B30 (G01 M40i/G45 M50) and B48B20 — the modular B-family engines are the modern gold standard
Pre-2015 N47D20C (E83/F25) and N20B20 (F25) without documented timing-chain replacement
2018+ G01 xDrive 20d or xDrive 20i with B47/B48 — post-chain-revision, CLAR platform, premium SUV packaging
G01 X3 M40i with B58 — 355–382 hp, the ultimate performance SUV before the full M
🏆 The Best-Selling BMW SUV — BMW's global volume leader and the most popular X model

ownershipVerdict.oneLineVerdict: The X3 is BMW's best-selling SUV — four generations, two decades, and one clear dividing line: the E83 and early F25's N47/N20 chain questions define the early cars; the G01 and G45's B-family engines define the modern ones. Buy on the generation line, not the badge.

rows.metric: Sub-headline: Four generations, 20+ years, and BMW's best-selling SUV. The right X3 depends on which generation and which engine you choose.
BMW X3 E83 🔥 Most Enquired — classic era [BMW-VERIFIED]

rows.value: Our Verdict: The original X3 — based on the E46/E90 3 Series platform with rear-wheel-drive as standard. The M54 and N52 straight-sixes are the robust picks; the N47 diesel carries the chain-tensioner caveat. The M57 3.0d is a bulletproof classic. Buy the six-cylinder or a diesel with documented chain history.

rows.metric: BMW X3 F25 🔥 Most Enquired — N47/N20 drives volume [BMW-VERIFIED]
rows.value: 2010–2017 • N20, N52, N55, N47, N57, B47 (LCI) — F10 5 Series platform

Our Verdict: The second-generation X3 — bigger, more refined, and built on the F10 5 Series platform. The N20 petrol and N47 diesel carry the chain-tensioner caveat; the N52 straight-six (early) and N55 are robust. The LCI (2015+) introduced the B47 diesel, finally fixing the chain issue. Buy 2015+ B47 or the N55.

rows.metric: BMW X3 G01 🔥 Most Enquired — B47/B48/B58 drives volume [BMW-VERIFIED]
rows.value: 2017–2024 • B47, B48, B57, B58, S58 (X3 M) — CLAR platform

Our Verdict: The third-generation X3 — CLAR platform, B-family engines only, and the most dependable X3 ever made. The B47 diesel and B48 petrol are the modern gold standard; the B58 in the M40i is a masterpiece; the S58 in the X3 M is the full M experience. This is the X3 generation to buy.
2024–present • B48, B58, B47 — CLAR platform, mild-hybrid, PHEV, iX3 electric

Our Verdict: The fourth-generation X3 — revealed 2024, available with petrol, diesel, mild-hybrid, plug-in hybrid, and fully electric iX3 powertrains. The B48 in the xDrive20 produces 208 hp; the B58 in the M50 xDrive produces 398 hp with 48V mild-hybrid assistance. Too new for volume reliability data, but the B-family record is impeccable.
The Generation Divide — The Line That Matters Most
Read the full comparison: BMW X3 F25 vs G01 →

rows.metric: The Badge Ambiguity — "20d" and "20i" Mean Different Things
rows.value: Correction applied (internal): The "20d" badge covers different engines depending on generation. On the E83 (2005–2010), it's the M47 or N47 — the chain-risk engine. On the F25 (2010–2014), it's the N47; on the F25 LCI (2015–2017), it's the B47. On the G01 (2018+), it's the B47. The "20i" badge covers the N20 (F25, chain risk) or B48 (G01/G45, robust). All share a badge. None share a reliability profile.

engineDatabase.h2: BMW X3 Engine Codes — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every engine ever fitted to a BMW X3 — verified codes, specifications and real UK ownership data.

confidenceScore.title: How confident are these ratings?
confidenceScore.text: N47D20, B47D20, B48B20 and B58B30 ratings: 90% confidence — high-volume engines with deep specialist rebuild history and clear failure patterns. M57D30 and N52B30 ratings: Expert-knowledge tier — decades of documented patterns from the E83/E90 era. E83 engines (M54, M47): Pattern-data tier — documented by specialist consensus. G45 engines: Provisional — the parc is too young for volume data; ratings reflect early patterns and shared-architecture evidence. Data note (internal) — corrections applied: Correction applied: The E83 X3 (2003–2010) used the M54 engine (2004–2006) and later the N52 (2006–2010) for petrol models. The diesel range included the M47 (2005–2007), N47 (2007–2010), and M57 (2003–2010). Correction applied: The F25 X3 (2010–2017) used the N20 petrol, N52 (early), N55, and the N47 diesel (2010–2014). The LCI (2015+) introduced the B47 diesel, replacing the N47. Correction applied: The G01 X3 (2017–2024) uses B-family engines only — B47, B48, B57, B58, and S58 (X3 M). No N-series engines. Correction applied: The G45 X3 (2024+) uses the B48B20P (xDrive20, 208 hp) and B58B30P (M50 xDrive, 398 hp). The B47D20B diesel is also available. Correction applied: The X3 M (F97) uses the S58 engine producing 473 hp (standard) or 503 hp (Competition).

commonProblems.subHeadline: The six most common failure patterns across the X3 range — symptoms, costs and urgency.

problems.description: The defining E83 and early F25 X3 issue — the N47 diesel's timing chain stretches, causing cold-start rattle. Ignored, chain slip destroys the engine. Affects E83 2.0d (2007–2010) and pre-2015 F25 20d. £3,200–£5,500 recon [BMW-QUOTE].
The N20 petrol's timing chain guide failure — plastic guides break, chain slips. Affects F25 20i/28i (2010–2017). Rattling on cold start is the warning. £2,800–£4,800 recon.
The B48's known weak points — coolant leaks from the water pump, oil filter housing, and heater management module. Affects G01 20i/30i and G45 models. £400–£800 repair if caught early.
The N47 diesel's EGR cooler can leak coolant, potentially causing fires. BMW issued recalls affecting 800,000 diesel engines. Affects E83 and F25 N47 diesels. Check recall history before buying.

The six-cylinder diesels' primary weak points — DPF clogging on urban-driven cars and injector issues. Affects E83 3.0d (M57) and F25 30d/35d (N57). Well-documented, repairable, but the M57 is far more robust than the N57.
Not an engine failure — but a common non-engine X3 enquiry. The E83 and F25's xDrive transfer case can fail, causing driveline vibration and noise. Check on any high-mileage E83/F25 xDrive.

marketIntelligence.h2: BMW X3 Market Intelligence — What UK Owners Told Us in 2025

signals.demandTrend: ⬇ Falling — the E83 is ageing out [PLACEHOLDER — YoY %]
⬆ Rising — the most popular X3 generation [PLACEHOLDER — YoY %]
⬆ Rising — new generation entering market [PLACEHOLDER — YoY %]
⬆ Rising — the N47's reputation drives due-diligence searches

insights: Interesting insight: The X3 generates a different kind of enquiry than the rest of the BMW range — owners are SUV buyers doing due diligence on specific known issues (N47 chain, N20 chain, B48 coolant) rather than performance enthusiasts. The X3 buyer is often a family buyer making a practical decision, making the chain question a critical decision point. The G01 with B47/B48 is the safe choice. (Editorial synthesis — specialist sense-check before publishing.)

The E83 X3 is now entering bargain territory — £3,500–£6,000 for a used example. But the N47 chain question means buyers need to budget accordingly: a £3,200–£5,500 recon on a £4,000 car is a hard decision. The M57 3.0d E83 is the robust alternative — but they're rare and hold their value. The M54 and N52 six-cylinder petrol E83s are also robust — but fuel economy is a consideration. (Editorial synthesis.)

The F25 X3 with the B47 diesel (2015+) or N55 petrol is the generation to buy on a budget — the B47 chain risk is gone, the F25 platform is proven, and the values are sensible (£8,000–£15,000). The G01 X3 with B47/B48/B58 is the generation to buy for the modern experience — the CLAR platform is the most refined, the B-family engines are the most dependable, and the values are holding strong (£15,000–£35,000). (Editorial synthesis.)

editorialPullQuote.title: The One Thing We'd Tell Every BMW X3 Buyer
editorialPullQuote.quote: If you buy one X3 based on this page, buy a 2018+ G01 with the B47 diesel or B48 petrol — the B-family engines fixed the chain issue that defined the E83 and early F25, the CLAR platform is the most refined X3 ever, and the values are holding strong. If the budget only stretches to an E83 or early F25, buy the M57 3.0d, the M54/N52 six-cylinder petrol, or insist on documented N47/N20 chain replacement — a £4,000 E83 without chain paperwork is the trap, not the bargain.

replacementCosts.h2: BMW X3 Engine Replacement Costs — By Generation
replacementCosts.subHeadline: Real UK pricing for used, reconditioned and rebuilt engines — supply only, with labour guidance.
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Labour estimate: Fitting a BMW X3 engine typically takes 10–20 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [BMW-QUOTE]. The X3 rule — the SUV premium: An N47/N20 recon at £3,200–£5,500 against a £4,000–£6,000 E83/F25 sits at 53–138% — ⚠️ watch to ❌ scrap. Against a £15,000–£25,000 G01, a B47/B48 recon at £2,800–£5,000 is 11–33% — ✅ safe. Against a £40,000 G01 M40i, a B58 recon at £3,800–£6,000 is 10–15% — ✅ safe. Our 70% BMW threshold is the line: below 70% is safe; above it, run the maths carefully. The G01 with B47/B48 is the one to buy; the E83/F25 N47/N20 is the one to avoid without chain paperwork.

Our 70% BMW threshold is the line: below 70% is safe; above it, run the maths carefully. The G01 with B47/B48 is the one to buy; the E83/F25 N47/N20 is the one to avoid without chain paperwork.

engineEvolution.h2: How the BMW X3 Engine Range Evolved — 2003 to Today

eras.whyBmwChanged: BMW launched the X3 as the compact SUV sibling to the X5. The M54 and N52 six-cylinder petrols and M57 six-cylinder diesel were the robust picks; the N47 diesel arrived later in the generation.
eras.worthKnowing: The E83 is the original X3 — the M57 3.0d is one of BMW's most durable diesels. The N47 carries the chain-tensioner caveat. Buy the six-cylinder or a diesel with documented chain history.

eras.whyBmwChanged: The second-generation X3 arrived on the F10 5 Series platform — bigger, more refined, and with a wider engine range. The LCI (2015+) introduced the B47 diesel, finally fixing the chain issue.
eras.worthKnowing: The N47/N20 timing chain issue defines the pre-LCI F25. The B47 in the LCI (2015+) is the one to buy. The N55 petrol is robust.

eras.whyBmwChanged: The third-generation X3 arrived on the CLAR platform — B-family engines only, no N-series carryover. The X3 M with the S58 arrived in 2019.
eras.worthKnowing: The G01 is the X3 to buy — B-family engines are the most dependable X3 powertrains ever made. The B58 in the M40i is a masterpiece; the S58 in the X3 M is the full M experience.

eras.whyBmwChanged: The fourth-generation X3 arrived with petrol, diesel, mild-hybrid, plug-in hybrid, and fully electric iX3 powertrains. The M50 xDrive produces 398 hp with 48V mild-hybrid assistance.
eras.worthKnowing: The G45 is the future — B-family engines with 48V mild-hybrid assistance. The iX3 is the first electric X3. Too new for volume reliability data, but the B-family record is impeccable.

whoShouldBuy.h2: Who Should Buy a BMW X3?

profiles.verdict: The X3 is the perfect family SUV — the G01 offers 550 litres of boot space, comfortable rear seats, and the B47 diesel delivers 55+ mpg. The G01 is the family X3 to buy; the G45 is the future.
The X3 is a great commuter — the G01 20d with B47 returns 55+ mpg, the CLAR platform is comfortable, and the SUV driving position is popular. The G45 is even more refined.

A cheap E83 or early F25 N47/N20 is not a cheap X3 — it's an expensive one with the first year's bills prepaid by nobody. A £8,000 F25 with B47 (2015+) is the bargain; a £4,000 E83 without chain paperwork is the trap.

The G01 X3 M40i with the B58 is a genuine performance SUV — 355–382 hp, xDrive, and the B58 is one of BMW's most reliable modern engines. The X3 M with the S58 is the full M experience — 473–503 hp and track-ready.
The E83 M57 3.0d is a classic keeper — one of BMW's most durable diesels. The G01 M40i with the B58 is the modern keeper — the B58 will outlast the car around it. Both are worth proper investment.

calculatorCta.intro: Cold-start rattle on an N47/N20? Coolant loss on a B48? On the X3, the answer depends on which generation you have — an E83/F25 and a G01/G45 face very different economics. Get a personalised repair-vs-replace verdict using real UK cost data.

items.question: How much does a BMW X3 engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A reconditioned BMW X3 engine typically costs £2,800–£5,500 supply only [BMW-QUOTE] — from the B47/B48 at £2,800–£5,000 to the N47/N20 at £3,200–£5,500. The B58 costs £3,800–£6,000; the S58 (X3 M) costs £7,000–£10,000+. Fitting adds £650–£1,500. Use our calculator for a personalised figure.

items.question: Which BMW X3 engine is the most reliable?
items.answer: The B58B30 straight-six in the G01 M40i and the B48B20 petrol in the G01/G45 — the B-family engines are the modern gold standard. The M57TUD30 in the E83 is the classic robust diesel pick.

items.question: Which BMW X3 engine should I avoid?
items.answer: No engine outright — but an E83 or pre-2015 F25 N47D20 or N20B20 without a documented timing-chain replacement is the one purchase we consistently advise against. A 2015+ F25 B47 or any G01 B-family engine has no such caveat.

items.question: How do I know if my X3 engine needs replacing?
items.answer: By engine: cold-start rattle (N47/N20 timing chain), chain noise under load (N47/N20), coolant loss (B48 cooling system), oil drips (N47 EGR cooler), DPF clogging (M57/N57). On the E83 and early F25, any rattling on startup is a warning sign. Caught early, it's a repair; ignored, it's a £2,800–£5,500 engine [BMW-QUOTE]. Use our diagnostic calculator for a personalised assessment.

items.question: Is it worth replacing a BMW X3 engine?
items.answer: Depends entirely on generation. Against a £15,000–£25,000 G01, a £3,000–£5,000 B47/B48 recon is 11–33% — ✅ safe. Against a £4,000–£6,000 E83/F25, a £3,200–£5,500 N47/N20 recon is 53–138% — ⚠️ watch to ❌ walk away. Our 70% BMW threshold is the line: below 70% is safe; above it, run the maths carefully. Use our calculator for your car.

items.question: Which BMW X3 should I buy?
items.answer: 2018+ G01 with the B47 diesel or B48 petrol — the B-family engines fixed the chain issue, the CLAR platform is the most refined X3 ever, and the values are holding strong (£15,000–£35,000). For performance, the G01 M40i with the B58 is the sweet spot. For the budget option, a 2015+ F25 with B47 diesel — the chain risk is gone, and the values are sensible (£8,000–£15,000). Avoid E83 and pre-2015 F25 N47/N20 without documented chain history.

items.question: How do I find my BMW X3 engine code?
items.answer: Listed on the VIN plate and in the vehicle documentation; block stamping is visible from below on most engines. Or tell us your generation and variant (E83 3.0d, F25 20d, G01 30i, G45 20d) and we'll identify it — 20+ vetted specialists will confirm it for free.

items.question: BMW X3 vs X1 — which is more reliable?
items.answer: Mechanically they share engines across similar eras — the N47/B47/B48 story is similar. The X3 is larger and heavier, so it works the powertrain harder; the X1 is smaller and lighter. Buy on engine and year, not badge. The X1 offers more value; the X3 offers more space and refinement. Read the full head-to-head →
